mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5 percent, it signals a structural labor market cooling that precedes corporate earnings recessions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative when UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent over a 60-day horizon, or the signal produces false positives in more than 40 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in housing starts and consumer credit indicates tightening financial conditions that suppress aggregate demand, mechanically reducing equity risk appetite and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The cross-product of monthly changes in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict 20-day S&P 500 returns with an information coefficient above 0.05 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In supply-constrained economic environments, rising industrial production directly translates to higher capacity utilization and expanded corporate operating margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns when supply chains are normalized, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product scales corporate earnings expectations directly, creating a persistent positive relationship between macroeconomic output expansion and equity valuations over the medium term.
failure_condition: Rolling three-year Spearman correlation between GDPC1 quarterly growth and 60-day S&P 500 returns remains consistently below 0.10 across all available data periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 325, inflation expectations become unanchored and force higher long-term discount rates, mechanically compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL crosses 325, indicating markets have fully priced in inflation or discount rate effects are offset by earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising employment and increasing unemployment signals structural labor supply shifts that suppress wage-driven inflation but indicate weak aggregate demand, negatively impacting cyclical equity valuations over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term constructed from monthly changes in PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no predictive power for 5-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward tests, or directional accuracy falls below chance.

mechanism_narrative: During rapid federal funds rate increases, rising housing starts acts as a leading indicator of consumer spending resilience that supports equity valuations despite higher borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns when FEDFUNDS rises, indicating rate sensitivity dominates housing demand signals in walk-forward validation.
